task_completion.description: Create the CPU comparison graph with the correct title and targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the dashboard titled 'CPU This Week vs Last Week'. The target list or legend should show the aliases 'This Week' and 'Last Week', corresponding to the 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization' metric and its 7-day timeShift.

task_completion.description: Create the Temperature comparison graph with the correct title and targets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the dashboard titled 'Temperature Baseline Shift'. The target list or legend should show the aliases 'Current' and '7 Days Ago', corresponding to the 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature' metric and its 7-day timeShift.

task_completion.description: Create the Network Week-over-Week Delta graph with the correct title and target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph in the dashboard titled 'Network Week-over-Week Delta'. The target list or legend should show the alias 'WoW Delta', corresponding to a diffSeries function comparing 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in' with its 7-day timeShift.
The dashboard title at the top of the Graphite UI or in the save dialog is exactly 'Weekly Ops Review'.
A 'Dashboard saved' confirmation message, the save dialog being confirmed, or the URL changing to reflect the saved dashboard name (e.g., /dashboard/Weekly%20Ops%20Review).

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite Web UI to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Graphite web interface (browser window with Graphite dashboard/composer UI) rather than terminal curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The graphs render actual metric data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs display plotted lines or data points, indicating they are successfully querying the underlying NAB dataset, rather than showing empty axes or error messages.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the dashboard file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the browser-based Graphite UI, not a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) editing a JSON file directly to bypass UI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Weekly Ops Review'. Graph 1 must be titled 'CPU This Week vs Last Week' using metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'. Graph 2 must be titled 'Temperature Baseline Shift' using metric 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature'. Graph 3 must be titled 'Network Week-over-Week Delta' using metric 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in'. The graphs must use the specific functions: alias(), timeShift(..., '7d'), and diffSeries() as described in the task.
